title: OTE Event
description: A single tech community event. See https://opentechevents.org for the normative prose.

allOf.description: A standalone event document must carry its own specVersion and license. Inside a feed, both are inherited from the feed.

specVersion.description: Version of OTE Spec this document adheres to.

id.description: Stable, globally unique identifier. A URI under a domain the publisher controls. Minted once, never rewritten — this is what lets consumers update an event instead of duplicating it.

url.description: Canonical URL where the event is described today. May change over time; id may not.

description.description: Short description. Plain text or Markdown.
examples: Charla introductoria a la programación asíncrona en Python, con ejemplos en vivo.

timezone.description: IANA timezone (e.g. Europe/Madrid). Turns a wall-clock startDate into an unambiguous instant. For all-day events it contextualises the date — it does not shift it.

startDate.description: Wall-clock start: a date (2026-10-15) for all-day events, or a local date-time (2026-10-15T09:00:00). Never carries a UTC offset — timezone does that.

endDate.description: Wall-clock end, in the SAME form as startDate (both dates, or both date-times). If absent, the event is assumed to end on the day it starts.

license.description: License of THIS DATA, not of the event. SPDX identifier (CC0-1.0, CC-BY-4.0…) or a URL.

examples.venue: Campus Madrid, Calle de Moreno Nieto 2, Madrid

attendanceMode.description: What the organiser says this event is. NO DEFAULT: absent means unknown, never in-person.

languages.description: BCP 47 tags, e.g. ["es","en"]. No default: absent means unknown.

tags.description: Free-form topic tags. Maps to iCal CATEGORIES and schema.org keywords. A controlled vocabulary may layer on top later; the field itself stays free. No default: absent means unknown.

status.description: A cancelled or postponed event MUST stay published: removing it leaves a dead event in subscribers' calendars.

source.description: Provenance. Required when the event was imported or aggregated from elsewhere; omitted when the organiser describes their own event — they are the source.

updatedAt.description: Instant the event's DATA last changed — equivalent to iCal LAST-MODIFIED, not DTSTAMP (which marks generation and changes on every export). Lets a consumer sync incrementally: fetch only what changed since its last read. Absent means unknown, not 'never changed'.

allOf.description: startDate and endDate must be of the same form: two all-day dates, or two local date-times.

instant.description: An absolute point in time, WITH offset or Z. Used for metadata (when data was fetched), never for when an event happens.

location.description: What is KNOWN about where the event happens. Not the same question as attendanceMode, which states the organiser's intent.

venue.description: Human-readable physical location. Its presence means the event has a physical venue.

onlineUrl.description: URL to attend online. Its presence means the event has online access.

geo.description: Coordinates of the physical venue (WGS-84 decimal degrees). Independent of venue, which is free text — a point, not a name. Maps to iCal GEO and schema.org Place.geo (GeoCoordinates).

url.description: Link to the original record, so the data can be verified and corrected upstream.

license.description: License under which the ORIGIN publishes the data. Constrains what may be republished: declaring a license does not grant rights the origin never gave.
